title: Clear 3 standing test failures: route-introspection + leaked Windows cwd (triage bucket A)
intent: Triage of the standing API-test failures (first visible after #166 cleared the radon collection crash) found a batch of pure test-side defects. Fix 3: (A1) two CAM intent-migration tests crash on fastapi>=0.137's _IncludedRouter route wrapper (no .path); (A2) a saddle-compensation CLI test hard-codes a Windows absolute cwd that does not exist on Ubuntu CI. Test-only; no production code, no routes, no behavior change. (Triage bucket B — a get_blueprint_adapter ImportError — was reclassified during grounding as a PRODUCTION regression in e2e_spine_runner.py and is NOT in this batch.)

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) test_drilling_intent_migration.py + test_pocketing_intent_migration.py: replace `{r.path for r in client.app.routes}` (which raised AttributeError: '_IncludedRouter' object has no attribute 'path' under fastapi>=0.137, the CI pin) with a `_route_paths(app)` helper that walks routes defensively — collects `.path` where present and recurses into nested `.routes`, skipping path-less wrappers. The asserted legacy CAM routes are real APIRoutes and are still captured. (2) test_saddle_compensation.py::test_cli_write_example_csv: replace a hard-coded `cwd="C:/Users/thepr/Downloads/luthiers-toolbox/services/api"` (a leaked Windows dev path; FileNotFoundError on Ubuntu CI) with `cwd=str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1])` (the services/api dir relative to the test file); add `from pathlib import Path`.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: These are real standing failures on main (15 surfaced after #166 fixed the collection crash), not flakes. Both are environment/version defects in the TESTS, not the product: the route-introspection breaks on the CI's fastapi version (local 0.136 lacks _IncludedRouter so it could not be reproduced locally — the recursive collector is correct by construction and is unit-proven against flat+nested+path-less route objects); the leaked Windows cwd can never resolve on a Linux runner. Fixing them shrinks the standing-red baseline with zero product risk and de-taxes future base-comparisons.

commands_run: services/api .venv: pytest tests/test_saddle_compensation.py::TestCsvRoundTrip::test_cli_write_example_csv -> 1 passed (102s; portable cwd works)
_route_paths unit-proven against synthetic routes: collects flat + nested(.routes) paths, skips path-less _IncludedRouter-like wrappers -> {drilling intent/gcode, pocketing intent} as expected
py_compile of all 3 edited test files -> OK
note: the two CAM migration tests use a full-app TestClient (local app import >2 min) so they are an Ubuntu-CI witness; the fix logic is witnessed locally

files.intent: Robust route-path collection (_route_paths) tolerant of fastapi>=0.137 _IncludedRouter wrappers
Portable subprocess cwd (services/api via __file__), replacing a leaked Windows absolute path
